raw_consumer_statement:
Why Im complaining XXXX transactions and/or practices were unfair or deceptive ( details below ). Despite timely disputes and new information, Amex did not conduct a reasonable investigation and relied on inadequate/incorrect merchant evidence. Blocking further review prevents correction of billing errors and leaves me without recourse. 

There are several hundred transactions with XXXX of my personal American Express accounts with an online casino. Total charges, upwards of {$100000.00} USD. Too many to list all dates and amounts here, but can provide separately if needed. 

My issue was " Product Service not as described ''. I believed the product/service purchased did not align with the product and service received. Additionally, this website called " XX/XX/XXXX '' appeared to operate as a legitimate casino, and ultimate found out they fly under the legal radar by claiming to be a 'XX/XX/XXXX '' ( which is now banned in many states ), where they bundle " XX/XX/XXXX '' ( GC ) which have no cash or entertainment value with " XX/XX/XXXX '' ( SC ) which have real monetary value. They also imply there is a legitimate " Return To Player '' ( XXXX ) metric they use, like real casinos that are monitored by governing bodies,, however the XXXX is a marketing play and not real, nor are they monitored by any legal governing agency. The GC 's I purchased were removed my account, and due to various other activities and reasons, I have lost in total of about {$250000.00} between Amex and other credit cards/debit cards. More details are in the attachments. 

I Filed a XX/XX/XXXXcomplaint with Amex, and was again denied my disputes, claiming the merchant provided supporting documentation. I am not disputing I made the purchases, I am disputing the I did not receive what I expected during my purchases. Amex has yet to provide copies from the merchant and will not allow me to dispute again, formally.
